text: Robotics world model reading club #01. Explicit 3D as the backbone: Towards unified world models for robotics.Join the [[discord](https://discord.gg/D8gyaVCP?utm_source=luma)]!
A catalog of JEPAs: 14 JEPA Milestones as a Map of AI Progress
Sebastian Rasbt just finished writing his latest book on building reasoning models from scratch
Some more details behind [Generalist's](https://generalistai.com/) live demo at GTC this year.

[Physical Intelligence](https://www.pi.website/) is in talks to [raise](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/27/physical-intelligence-is-reportedly-in-talks-to-raise-1-billion-again/) **$1B** at an **$11B** valuation! 😲

![Physical Intelligence](https://www.314159.com/images/og/og-home.png)

This GTC '26 session explores how NVIDIA's Alpamayo--particularly reasoning vision-language-action models (VLA)--enable vehicles to interpret, reason about, and navigate complex real-world scenarios with greater humanlike understanding.

How [Ilia Larchenko](https://huggingface.co/IliaLarchenko) et al. trained a robot to do 50 household tasks in simulation by building upon the [$\pi_{0.5}$](https://www.pi.website/blog/pi05) VLA architecture [[Code](https://github.com/IliaLarchenko/behavior-1k-solution)] [[Paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.06951)] [[Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J4wpO0EdCZs)]

NVIDIA's [Openpi Comet](https://github.com/mli0603/openpi-comet) came in second place, but they were able to push performance significantly higher on the public validation set after the fact.
Latest blog post from [Chris Paxton](https://cpaxton.github.io/about/) -- three kinds of world models, what they are, and why they matter

**Dream2Flow**, a framework that bridges video generation and robotic control through 3D object flow as an intermediate representation. This method reconstructs 3D object motions from generated videos and formulates manipulation as object trajectory tracking.
Looks like TRI is building an open source VLA training framework.

Here is [Cheng Chi](https://cheng-chi.github.io/)'s guest lecture from [Oier Mees](https://www.oiermees.com/) Robot Learning [course](https://cvg.ethz.ch/lectures/Robot-Learning/) at ETHZ (2026) on **Robotics Beyond Algorithms**. In this talk he covers practical tips and tricks of building real-world robotic systems.
A follow-up to Sebastian Raschka's [LLM architecture gallery](https://sebastianraschka.com/llm-architecture-gallery/) covering all different variants of attention mechanisms.
Starting a new page for shorter-form writing. Not blog posts, just quick observations, interesting links, and things I'm thinking about.
